BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of the sometimes strong wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of a moderate to strong southwesterly wind, further wind slabs will form in the course of the day. They can be released by a single winter sport participant and reach large size. This applies in particular on steep slopes above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Down to low altitudes snow will fall from the afternoon. The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for caution and restraint.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Down to low altitudes snow will fall from the afternoon. As a consequence of the moderate to strong southwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form.
The snowpack is soft; its surface consists of loosely bonded snow. This applies in shady places that are protected from the wind at low and intermediate altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and wind.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of the sometimes strong wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of a moderate to strong southwesterly wind, further wind slabs will form in the course of the day. They can be released by a single winter sport participant and reach large size. This applies in particular on steep slopes above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Down to low altitudes snow will fall from the afternoon. The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for caution and restraint.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Down to low altitudes snow will fall from the afternoon. As a consequence of the moderate to strong southwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form.
The snowpack is soft; its surface consists of loosely bonded snow. This applies in shady places that are protected from the wind at low and intermediate altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and wind.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Dry slab avalanches are still possible. Avalanches can be released in near-ground layers by small loads. Remotely triggered and natural avalanches are possible. In addition the fresh and older wind slabs must be taken into account. Sometimes the avalanches are large.
Whumpfing sounds serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: New snow and wind slabs are lying mostly on old snow containing large grains. The avalanche-prone wind slabs are lying on weak layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes above approximately 1500 m. Avalanches can be released by small loads.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The natural avalanche activity will gradually decrease.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights:
Considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
In particular in regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations are more widespread.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack. They can in many places be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and whumpfing sounds can indicate the danger. Reports have been received that some forested areas have been affected by avalanche activity and may also present unstable conditions on steep slopes. Great caution and restraint are required.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The snowpack remains in most cases unstable.
Over a wide area precarious wind slabs formed. Numerous weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be very cloudy. Over a wide area snowfall.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: At elevated altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail. As a consequence of the sometimes moderate wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Some snow will fall in the afternoon. The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. Wind slabs can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant and reach large size. This applies in particular on steep slopes also above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.
Avalanches can be released in deeper layers in particular on steep shady slopes. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches are a clear indication of a weakly bonded snowpack.
Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
As a consequence of wind, further wind slabs will form. The new snow and wind slabs will be deposited on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: At elevated altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail. As a consequence of the moderate to strong wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent as the day progresses.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In particular on steep slopes and adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches are possible as a consequence of the moderate wind.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases.
In particular on very steep shady slopes the avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach large size.
Some snow will fall from the afternoon.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The moderate wind will transport the snow. The fresh wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.

Faceted weak layers exist in the snowpack on steep shady slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution. As a consequence of new snow and wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent in the evening.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh snow from last week, and especially the wind-blown snow drifts, both soft and hard, cover a thin layer of old snow. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches as before, including medium-sized ones. Mostly they are shallow, caution is to be exercised in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, and on very steep slopes.
On very steep shady slopes the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases. Slopes that have been little used this winter thus far where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially unfavourable. Isolated whumpfing sounds and shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.
Some snow will fall in the evening. The wind will be moderate to strong. These meteorological conditions will prevent a change towards better conditions.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from northwesterly directions, wind slabs formed above the tree line.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
As a consequence of solar radiation a crust formed on the surface during the last few days, in particular on very steep south facing slopes.
Since Thursday, numerous spontaneous and triggered slab avalanches have been observed, especially in areas bordering Switzerland and France.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The wind will be moderate to strong. Some snow will fall until midday. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from southeasterly directions, further wind slabs will form.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of the sometimes strong wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent as the day progresses.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of a sometimes moderate wind, further wind slabs will form in the course of the day. They can be released, even by small loads in isolated cases and reach dangerously large size. This applies in particular on steep slopes above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Some snow will fall in the afternoon. The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for defensive route selection.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The moderate wind will transport the snow. The fresh wind slabs will be deposited on unfavourable layers.

The snowpack is soft; its surface consists of loosely bonded snow. This applies in shady places that are protected from the wind also at low and intermediate altitudes.
Faceted weak layers exist in the snowpack on steep shady slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of the sometimes strong wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent as the day progresses.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of a sometimes moderate wind, further wind slabs will form in the course of the day. They can be released, even by small loads in isolated cases and reach dangerously large size. This applies in particular on steep slopes above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Some snow will fall in the afternoon. The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for defensive route selection.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The moderate wind will transport the snow. The fresh wind slabs will be deposited on unfavourable layers.

The snowpack is soft; its surface consists of loosely bonded snow. This applies in shady places that are protected from the wind also at low and intermediate altitudes.
Faceted weak layers exist in the snowpack on steep shady slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.highlights: The conditions are very precarious for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Outside marked and open pistes a very precarious avalanche situation will prevail.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh snow as well as the widespread wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. Avalanches can be released easily and reach medium size. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

The avalanche prone locations are widespread and are clearly recognisable to the trained eye. The number and size of avalanche prone locations will increase with altitude. Caution is to be exercised in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
Field observations and avalanches triggered by explosives confirm the unfavourable bonding of the snowpack. Caution and restraint are recommended.
Slight increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of the precipitation.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies especially on west, north and east facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Slight increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of the snowfall. Avalanches can as before be released, even by a single winter sport participant. The snowpack remains prone to triggering.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: New snow and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry avalanches have been released in the last few days. Natural avalanches are still even now possible. The fresh snow and the wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. Especially here avalanches can be triggered in the faceted old snow. This applies even in case of a small load. Wind slabs are covered with new snow and therefore difficult to recognise. The avalanche prone locations are widespread and are barely recognisable. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. The number and size of avalanche prone locations will increase with altitude. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches serve as an alarm sign. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection. In particular in regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent and the danger is greater. The snow sport conditions outside marked and open pistes are dangerous.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The snowpack will be in most cases prone to triggering. Over a wide area new snow is lying on old snow containing large grains. This applies especially in shady places that are protected from the wind. The old snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Hardly any decrease in avalanche danger. The current avalanche situation calls for caution and restraint.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: A treacherous avalanche situation will persist. Weakly bonded old snow represents the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh snow as well as the widespread wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line, in isolated cases also in areas close to the tree line, and on steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. Avalanches can be released easily and reach medium size. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

The avalanche prone locations are widespread and are barely recognisable. The number and size of avalanche prone locations will increase with altitude. Caution is to be exercised adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger.

Field observations and fresh avalanches confirm a treacherous avalanche situation. Caution and restraint are recommended.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies especially on west, north and east facing slopes.

On steep sunny slopes the snowpack will freeze but not form a strong crust, in particular below approximately 2400 m.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released by individual winter sport participants. In particular in the south up to 15 cm of snow will fall. The sometimes storm force wind will transport the fresh and old snow significantly. The avalanche activity will increase.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Individual weak layers exist in the top section of the old snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The wind slabs of last week are poorly bonded with the old snowpack in particular on northwest to north to east facing aspects above approximately 1800 m. Individual weak layers exist in the top section of the old snowpack. Below approximately 1800 m mostly small moist snow slides and avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: As a consequence of the strong wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. The wind slabs have bonded with the old snowpack. They are to be assessed with care and prudence. In addition further wind slabs formed in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Weak layers in the old snowpack indicate that the stability of the snowpack varies greatly within a small area.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Avalanches can in isolated cases be released, but they will be small in most cases.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can in isolated cases be released, but they will be small in most cases. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep shady slopes, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line.

Apart from the danger of being buried, restraint should be exercised in particular in view of the danger of avalanches sweeping people along and giving rise to falls.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The rather small wind slabs of the last few days are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack at elevated altitudes. The old snowpack consists of faceted crystals.

The snowpack will be generally subject to considerable local variations.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Dry slab avalanches are possible. Sometimes the avalanches are medium-sized and can be released in some cases even by a single winter sport participant. Avalanches can be released in near-ground layers by small loads. In addition the fresh and older wind slabs must be taken into account. In isolated cases the avalanches are large.
Whumpfing sounds serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: In some cases new snow and wind slabs are lying on old snow containing large grains. The avalanche-prone wind slabs are lying on weak layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes above approximately 1500 m. Avalanches can be released by small loads.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The natural avalanche activity will gradually decrease.

BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The weather conditions gave rise to significant settling of the snowpack.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Dry loose snow avalanches are unlikely to occcur. Individual gliding avalanches can also occur. The avalanches are only small and can only be released by large loads.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Areas with glide cracks are to be avoided. Above approximately 1000 m a little snow is lying.
